title: pimpinella kingdon-wardii
intro: Pimpinella kingdon-wardii, also known as Kingdon-Ward's carrot, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae. It is primarily found in the Himalayas and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its carminative and expectorant properties. Pimpinella kingdon-wardii can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
